response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine systems-programming depth, but the role's core requirement is hands-on modern C++ for industrial automation, and his C++ work dates back to roughly 2008 (National Instruments, bachelor thesis) and does not even appear in his current skills list. His recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js web and platform work plus DLang, with no evidence of MQTT, IoT protocols, industrial cybersecurity, or IEC 62443/SDL practice. The low-latency, event-driven experience is transferable in spirit, but the gap on the central competencies is too large for a senior role expected to architect and mentor in C++.
strengths: Real high-performance systems experience: low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec using async I/O, caching, and fibers
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing record (workshops, multi-module courses, coding dojos)
Broad full-stack versatility with a testing and software-craftsmanship mindset (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and ~18 years old; C++ is absent from his stated skills
No industrial automation, MQTT/IoT, or fieldbus protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
No exposure to Secure Development Lifecycle or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity standards

key_factors.factor: Industrial IoT and security standards experience
